jokes.text: My name is Kevin, or so they tell me.
jokes.setup: Documentary-style introduction where we expect normal self-identification
jokes.punchline: Amnesia sufferer doesn't even know his own name, delivered with documentary earnestness
jokes.explanation: Strong character-driven opener that establishes the central premise with perfect delivery. The formal documentary tone contrasts beautifully with the absurd content.

jokes.text: But by using a special, shiny glass called a mirror, I've estimated that I'm 15 years old, although I have no way of knowing.
jokes.setup: Kevin explains his memory loss condition in documentary style
jokes.punchline: Describes a mirror as 'special, shiny glass' and estimates his clearly adult age as 15
jokes.explanation: Brilliant character comedy that establishes Kevin's childlike worldview while being absurd enough to hint at the con. The mirror description is perfect economy of absurdism.

jokes.text: why Changnesia affects the memory but not the ability to make forced puns...
jokes.setup: Kevin lists symptoms of his condition
jokes.punchline: Self-aware reference to Chang's notorious punning habit
jokes.explanation: Perfect meta-joke that acknowledges Chang's character history while maintaining the Kevin persona. High character integration.

jokes.text: Then he became a disgraced student, psychopathic music major, homeless vent dweller, security guard, keytarist, power hungry warlord, and now, Kevin.
jokes.setup: Dean recounts Chang's employment history
jokes.punchline: Increasingly absurd job titles culminating in 'power hungry warlord'
jokes.explanation: Excellent escalation structure that serves as series recap while building to absurd climax. Good economy.

jokes.text: I'm developing a theory which some find controversial. Perhaps Chang actually used to be Kevin, went crazy, and became Chang. And then, at some point, Chang hit his head, went un-crazy, thereby reverting back to regular old Kevin again.
jokes.setup: Dean presents a medical theory about Kevin's condition
jokes.punchline: Completely nonsensical reverse-psychology theory with 'went un-crazy'
jokes.explanation: Solid absurdist logic that captures Dean's pseudo-intellectual nature, though slightly wordy for the payoff.

jokes.text: That makes no sense. Science tells us hitting his head would only cure him if hitting his head was the original cause.
jokes.setup: Dean's absurd medical theory
jokes.punchline: Annie's matter-of-fact medical correction delivered seriously
jokes.explanation: Perfect Annie response - earnest, logical correction that highlights the absurdity through contrast.

jokes.text: so we can continue to fight this terrible disease and hopefully, one day, pay for this documentary.
jokes.setup: Dean making impassioned plea for grant money
jokes.punchline: Casually mentions paying for the documentary as secondary goal
jokes.explanation: Clever meta-joke about documentary funding that breaks the fourth wall subtly.

jokes.text: How was that, Abed? That's too beggy? No, that was great. But I could use a new camera, with a smoother zoom.
jokes.setup: Dean asks for feedback on his grant pitch
jokes.punchline: Abed immediately uses it as opportunity to ask for better equipment
jokes.explanation: Perfect Abed moment - focused on production values over content, very character-specific.

jokes.text: You gotta be fucking kidding me.
jokes.setup: Abed tells Jeff he's making a documentary about Changnesia
jokes.punchline: Jeff's immediate, exasperated profanity
jokes.explanation: Simple but effective reaction that establishes Jeff's stance. Perfect economy but not particularly original.

jokes.text: I'm telling you, we should have sprung for the name brand glitter.
jokes.setup: Group making banners for the documentary
jokes.punchline: Britta's misplaced priorities on glitter quality
jokes.explanation: Solid character moment showing Britta's tendency to focus on wrong details.

jokes.text: The history of ice cream class had another heart attack. Three more, and we're all in.
jokes.setup: Troy reporting on class availability
jokes.punchline: Casually counting heart attacks needed to get into class
jokes.explanation: Dark humor perfectly delivered with casual tone. Great economy and structure.

jokes.text: He kept the dean in a dungeon. He tried to kill us. He ate his twin in utero.
jokes.setup: Jeff listing Chang's past crimes
jokes.punchline: Escalates from real crimes to absurd prenatal cannibalism
jokes.explanation: Excellent escalation that starts grounded and becomes hilariously absurd. The in utero detail is perfect absurdist topper.

jokes.text: Whoa. This makes things smaller. No, no... Bigger. Oh, bigger.
jokes.setup: Kevin examining something (likely magnifying glass)
jokes.punchline: Childlike wonder at discovering magnification
jokes.explanation: Good character work showing Kevin's childlike perspective, though simple concept.

jokes.text: Well, enjoy your collective delusion. I'm going to go get a sandwich, which, unlike Changnesia, is real.
jokes.setup: Jeff dismissing the group's belief in Kevin
jokes.punchline: Contrasts sandwich reality with Changnesia fiction
jokes.explanation: Strong sarcastic delivery that's very Jeff. Good contrast structure.

jokes.text: Welcome to Shirley's sandwiches, where the food is Kevin-ly.
jokes.setup: Shirley's business slogan
jokes.punchline: Kevin-ly as pun on 'heavenly'
jokes.explanation: Forced pun typical of Shirley's business attempts. Serviceable but not particularly clever.

jokes.text: Hi, not calling you Kevin.
jokes.setup: Jeff says 'Not calling you Kevin'
jokes.punchline: Kevin treats it as Jeff's actual name
jokes.explanation: Brilliant wordplay that shows Kevin's literal interpretation. Perfect economy and character integration.

jokes.text: Like how I gave Andre a second chance. It doesn't mean I forgot. I just forgave.
jokes.setup: Shirley explaining second chances
jokes.punchline: Comparing Chang to her cheating ex-husband
jokes.explanation: Good callback that uses Shirley's personal history to make a point about forgiveness

jokes.text: What Shirley said really made me think. I mean, all my friends believe this guy. What does it say about me that I don't?
jokes.setup: Jeff in confessional claiming to reconsider his position
jokes.punchline: Mock sincerity while planning to expose Chang
jokes.explanation: Good setup for Jeff's deception, works better on rewatch knowing his true motives.

jokes.text: What does it say? It says I have gullible friends who will believe anybody, and I need to make that anybody me. By exposing Chang in front of the whole school.
jokes.setup: Jeff's internal monologue revealing his true plan
jokes.punchline: Admits to manipulating his friends' gullibility
jokes.explanation: Solid character moment revealing Jeff's cynical manipulation. Very character-specific.

jokes.text: Much like the classic documentary Capturing the Friedmans, my story just took a completely unexpected turn.
jokes.setup: Abed commenting on the documentary's direction
jokes.punchline: Overly academic film reference for personal drama
jokes.explanation: Perfect Abed - treating personal moments through film theory lens. High character integration.

jokes.text: Jeff, if you want this done right, I'm going to need a steadicam for Garrett.
jokes.setup: Jeff wanting to expose Chang
jokes.punchline: Abed immediately asking for more expensive equipment
jokes.explanation: Good callback showing Abed's consistent priorities as a filmmaker

jokes.text: I've got a blackface senor Wences bit I've been workshopping.
jokes.setup: Pierce volunteering to host reception
jokes.punchline: Horrifically inappropriate comedy bit
jokes.explanation: Perfect Pierce - completely tone-deaf and offensive. The cringe is the comedy.

jokes.text: I'm going to call you 'Houlihan.' 'Partner' and 'Houlihan.'
jokes.setup: Troy and Annie forming investigative partnership
jokes.punchline: Troy's arbitrary detective name assignment
jokes.explanation: Good character moment showing Troy's childlike approach to detective work.

jokes.text: In an investigation, one dude always has to go opposite the other dude. That's how they get things done. Well, you guys can work it out. Yeah, we will. No, we won't.
jokes.setup: Troy explaining detective dynamics
jokes.punchline: Immediately demonstrates by contradicting Annie
jokes.explanation: Excellent meta-joke about detective tropes with immediate demonstration. Good structure and economy.

jokes.text: [Chang recites entire conversation verbatim with perfect recall]
jokes.setup: Jeff mentions Chang having good memory for a lawyer comment
jokes.punchline: Kevin recites entire lengthy conversation word-for-word
jokes.explanation: Brilliant payoff that reveals Chang's deception while maintaining Kevin persona. Perfect callback structure and character work.

jokes.text: Well, my short term memory has actually improved. My doctor calls it a, um... side effect.
jokes.setup: Jeff questioning Kevin's perfect memory
jokes.punchline: Convenient medical excuse that doesn't make logical sense
jokes.explanation: Good recovery showing Chang thinking on his feet while maintaining the con. The pause before 'side effect' suggests improvisation.

jokes.text: Well played, Chang. The chess match has begun. What... I'm Chinese?
jokes.setup: Jeff realizes Chang is playing him
jokes.punchline: Kevin's innocent confusion about being called Chinese
jokes.explanation: Perfect contrast between Jeff's awareness and Kevin's maintained innocence. Great timing and character work.

jokes.text: An 'entrepreneu-her,' if you will. Or maybe a businesswoman? Okay, fine. Think inside the box.
jokes.setup: Britta pitching her documentary angle
jokes.punchline: Defensive about her forced portmanteau being rejected
jokes.explanation: Very Britta - trying too hard to be clever with wordplay and getting defensive when corrected.

jokes.text: What you talkin' about, hookey? I haven't introduced you to my Asian wife yet. Uh-oh... I was wrong. [Pierce gets worse]
jokes.setup: Pierce performing his offensive comedy bit
jokes.punchline: Each line becomes progressively more offensive
jokes.explanation: Effective cringe comedy that's painful to watch. The escalation works for character but comedy is deliberately bad.

jokes.text: Garrett, here he comes. Get up. I can't. It's too heavy! Garrett, now. Now.
jokes.setup: Annie and Garrett trying to film with steadicam
jokes.punchline: Garrett can't lift the heavy camera equipment
jokes.explanation: Simple physical comedy that works in context but isn't particularly memorable.

jokes.text: This is Houlihan. I'm her partner, Partner. Wait, I'm Partner. No, you're not.
jokes.setup: Troy and Annie's detective name confusion
jokes.punchline: Immediate confusion about who has which name
jokes.explanation: Good callback that shows the arbitrary nature of their detective roleplay.

jokes.text: I'm the last face that a lot of trout ever see. I'm not saying that the trout are a vengeful breed, but if they were, I'd probably be public enemy number one.
jokes.setup: Sullivan explains seeing something emerge from trout tank
jokes.punchline: Paranoid theory about vengeful trout seeking revenge
jokes.explanation: Excellent character comedy with absurdist logic. Great specificity about trout vengeance makes it memorable.

jokes.text: I was pretty relieved when I found out it was a naked Asian guy, and not an angry trout.
jokes.setup: Sullivan's fear of vengeful trout
jokes.punchline: Relief that it was just a naked man, not a fish
jokes.explanation: Perfect callback that inverts normal priorities - naked man less concerning than angry fish.

jokes.text: What do I look like, trout world? No employees. Heh, just me. And my dog.
jokes.setup: Asked about employees at trout farm
jokes.punchline: Dismissive 'trout world' reference and casual dog mention
jokes.explanation: Good character work showing Sullivan's folksy manner and casual approach to business.

jokes.text: What do I look like, trout world? No employees. Heh, just me. And my dog.
jokes.setup: Asked about employees at his trout farm
jokes.punchline: Defensive response comparing himself to SeaWorld, then admitting to having a dog
jokes.explanation: Good character consistency showing Sullivan's defensiveness and humble operation.

jokes.text: A spike in production in June, at the peak of milkweed allergy season? Something on this trout farm was starting to smell... wrong.
jokes.setup: Annie analyzing Sullivan's story like a detective
jokes.punchline: Overly dramatic detective narration about mundane details
jokes.explanation: Good parody of detective genre conventions with Annie's over-analytical approach.

jokes.text: Well, looks like everything checks out here. Right, Troy? No, Houlihan, I don't think anything checks out here.
jokes.setup: Annie trying to leave with information
jokes.punchline: Troy immediately contradicts per detective rules
jokes.explanation: Perfect callback to Troy's detective rule, executed immediately and automatically.

jokes.text: Fine! You got me. I've been using that Asian guy as unpaid, manual labor for three months before I drove him into town. But he wanted me to! We were friends, and I treated him with respect and dignity.
jokes.setup: Sullivan breaking under pressure
jokes.punchline: Admits to labor exploitation while claiming friendship and dignity
jokes.explanation: Good ironic contrast between exploitation and claimed respectful treatment.

jokes.text: Fine! You got me. I've been using that Asian guy as unpaid, manual labor for three months before I drove him into town. But he wanted me to!
jokes.setup: Good cop/bad cop routine on Sullivan
jokes.punchline: Immediate confession followed by absurd justification
jokes.explanation: Good escalation from defensive to confession, plus the absurd 'but he wanted me to' defense.

jokes.text: Growing up, I had a cat named Troy, a bird named Troy, and a hamster named Troy. They were all older than me.
jokes.setup: Discussion about Kevin being named after a dog
jokes.punchline: Troy's pattern of being named after pets, all older than him
jokes.explanation: Brilliant Troy backstory that's both absurd and oddly touching. Perfect escalating structure and character specificity.

jokes.text: They were supposed to come back with a smoking gun, and all they found was a little human rights abuse. Which, normally, is terrible.
jokes.setup: Jeff frustrated with investigation results
jokes.punchline: Dismisses human rights abuse as insufficient for his needs
jokes.explanation: Dark comedy that shows Jeff's moral flexibility perfectly. Great economy and character integration.

jokes.text: ♪ The itsy-bitsy spider went up the water spout
jokes.setup: Footage of Kevin alone in his apartment
jokes.punchline: Adult man earnestly singing children's song
jokes.explanation: Decent character consistency showing Kevin's childlike nature, though not particularly inventive.

jokes.text: It goes on like this for another 12 hours. I couldn't even get through it all, and I sat through The Tree of Life.
jokes.setup: Jeff reviewing Britta and Shirley's footage
jokes.punchline: Film reference comparing bad footage to notoriously slow art film
jokes.explanation: Solid pop culture reference that works for character and situation, though not exceptional.

jokes.text: When he's alone, he mostly just practices smiling and frowning. We've all done it.
jokes.setup: Discussing Kevin's private behavior
jokes.punchline: Casual admission that everyone practices facial expressions
jokes.explanation: Good observational humor that feels true to life. Perfect Abed delivery.

jokes.text: You shut up. But I love you. You get yourself in the kitchen, and make me a burrito. Oh, okay, yay! Oh, thank you.
jokes.setup: Pierce performing his offensive bit
jokes.punchline: Kevin's enthusiastic response to being ordered around
jokes.explanation: Effective cringe showing both Pierce's awfulness and Kevin's innocent enthusiasm.

jokes.text: So please welcome to the stage the former Mrs. Ben Chang, Alessandra.
jokes.setup: Jeff setting up his trap for Chang
jokes.punchline: Reveal of Chang's ex-wife as the smoking gun
jokes.explanation: Strong dramatic reveal that serves as climax of Jeff's plan. Good structure and buildup.

jokes.text: You called her over and over and over again, even though you don't know her or how to use a phone or what a number is.
jokes.setup: Jeff revealing Chang's phone calls
jokes.punchline: Lists increasingly basic things Kevin supposedly doesn't understand
jokes.explanation: Good escalating structure that builds the logical trap effectively.

jokes.text: I found it in the vents! It's 555-0190. And it was the most beautiful thing I'd ever seen.
jokes.setup: Kevin explaining how he got the phone number
jokes.punchline: Finding beauty in a random phone number written down
jokes.explanation: Good character moment showing Kevin's innocent wonder at mundane things

jokes.text: I found it in the vents! It's 555-0190. And it was the most beautiful thing I'd ever seen. But I always hung up, because... I didn't know if the person on the other end would ever want to talk to Kevin... after knowing Chang.
jokes.setup: Kevin explaining the phone calls
jokes.punchline: Touching explanation that maintains Kevin persona while admitting Chang knowledge
jokes.explanation: Brilliant character work that maintains ambiguity - could be genuine or manipulation. Very touching either way.

jokes.text: Look, if he doesn't remember her, then this wouldn't bother him. My logic is flawless! Doesn't that bother you? That bothers you!
jokes.setup: Jeff's plan backfiring as Kevin seems genuine
jokes.punchline: Increasingly desperate attempts to prove his point
jokes.explanation: Good cringe comedy showing Jeff's desperation as his plan fails publicly.

jokes.text: Hey, Alessandra, his last girlfriend was a charred mannequin leg!
jokes.setup: Jeff desperately trying to expose Chang
jokes.punchline: Reveals bizarre detail about Chang's romantic life
jokes.explanation: Hilariously specific and absurd detail that's both shocking and perfect for Chang's character. Great economy.

jokes.text: On behalf of the Macguffin neurological institute, Grant granted.
jokes.setup: Jeff's public meltdown was supposed to discredit Changnesia
jokes.punchline: Instead convinces grant committee that it's a serious problem
jokes.explanation: Perfect ironic reversal of Jeff's intentions. Great structure and economy.

jokes.text: Grant granted.
jokes.setup: Deciding to give Greendale the grant
jokes.punchline: Efficient pun announcing the decision
jokes.explanation: Simple but effective pun with perfect economy. Works as official bureaucratic language.

jokes.text: By trying to make Kevin a pariah, I accidentally became more Chang than Chang at his Changiest.
jokes.setup: Jeff reflecting on his behavior
jokes.punchline: Admits to out-Changing Chang with Chang pun
jokes.explanation: Good self-reflection that includes appropriate Chang pun while showing character growth.

jokes.text: Well, I didn't try to kill anyone. Which no one seems to care about. But you know what I'm saying.
jokes.setup: Jeff comparing himself to Chang
jokes.punchline: Defensive qualification about not being murderous
jokes.explanation: Good character moment showing Jeff's defensiveness and maintaining callback to Chang's crimes.

jokes.text: Wanting to leave that name behind is the sanest decision anyone can make.
jokes.setup: Jeff understanding what it feels like to be Chang
jokes.punchline: Admits Chang's name is worth abandoning
jokes.explanation: Good character growth moment showing Jeff's newfound empathy and self-awareness.

jokes.text: A lot of filmmakers use crane shots to elicit emotion. What the heck, Abed?
jokes.setup: Emotional reconciliation scene
jokes.punchline: Abed breaks the moment with technical film commentary
jokes.explanation: Perfect Abed moment - disrupting emotion with film analysis. High character integration and meta-commentary.

jokes.text: Why can't you ever make a documentary about the thing you plan to make a documentary about?
jokes.setup: Annie frustrated with Abed's documentary habits
jokes.punchline: Calls out Abed's tendency to hijack documentary subjects
jokes.explanation: Smart meta-commentary on the episode's structure and Abed's character patterns. Works on multiple levels.

jokes.text: Hi, it's me. They all finally bought it. Hook, line, and Winger. I patiently await your further instructions. Chang out.
jokes.setup: Episode seems to end with ambiguity about Kevin's authenticity
jokes.punchline: Reveals Chang was faking all along with fishing pun on Winger
jokes.explanation: Perfect ending reveal that recontextualizes entire episode. Great pun work and dramatic structure. Classic Community twist.

jokes.text: Why did I do that? Man...
jokes.setup: Chang just revealed his deception
jokes.punchline: Immediately regrets evil monologuing
jokes.explanation: Perfect coda showing Chang's self-awareness about villain monologue trope. Great economy and character moment.
